Class DashboardController

java.lang.Object
Logic.DashboardController
public class DashboardController extends Object
Controller class responsible for handling dashboard-related operations.

This class acts as an intermediary between the presentation layer and the persistence layer by retrieving and processing financial data required for the dashboard.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    DashboardController()
    Constructs a DashboardController object and initializes the persistence layer object.

    Retrieves the total expenses grouped by category.

    Retrieves the remaining balance available in the current budget cycle.

    Retrieves the number of remaining days in the current budget cycle.

    Retrieves the safe daily spending limit for the user.

  • Constructor Details

    • DashboardController

      public DashboardController()
      Constructs a DashboardController object and initializes the persistence layer object.

  • Method Details

    • getSafeDailyLimit

      public double getSafeDailyLimit()
      Retrieves the safe daily spending limit for the user.
      Returns:
      the safe daily spending limit

    • getRemainingBalance

      public double getRemainingBalance()
      Retrieves the remaining balance available in the current budget cycle.
      Returns:
      the remaining balance

    • getRemainingDays

      public long getRemainingDays()
      Retrieves the number of remaining days in the current budget cycle.
      Returns:
      the remaining number of days

    • isFinalDay

      public boolean isFinalDay()
      Checks whether the current day is the final day of the budget cycle.
      Returns:
      true if it is the final day, otherwise false

    • getCategoryTotals

      public Map<category,Double> getCategoryTotals()
      Retrieves the total expenses grouped by category.
      Returns:
      a map containing each category and its total expense amount

    • getCategoryPercentages

      public Map<category,Double> getCategoryPercentages()
      Calculates the percentage of total expenses for each category.

      The percentage is calculated using the formula:

      :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
      Returns:
      a map containing each category and its percentage contribution to the total expenses
